tasks.context:
Update backend/src/routes/invoices.ts to use the new chain-validator module.

The file currently has a hardcoded SUPPORTED_CHAINS validation. You need to make exactly these changes:

1. Add import at top (after existing imports):
   import { validateChain } from ../lib/chain-validator;

2. Find any line like:
   const SUPPORTED_CHAINS = [...]
   or chain validation inline code and replace with:
   const chain = validateChain(req.body.chain ?? req.query.chain);

3. Remove the old SUPPORTED_CHAINS constant if it exists.

CRITICAL RULES:
- Do NOT rewrite the whole file from scratch
- Keep ALL existing route handlers exactly as they are
- Only touch the import section and the chain validation line
- The file must remain fully functional with all original endpoints intact
- Output the COMPLETE file (all 389 lines approximately), modifying only those 3 spots
- If the file seems too long, add a comment // [rest of file unchanged] at line 150 — the supervisor will reject truncation anyway

review.summary: Manually verified: validateChain correctly imported and used at lines 97, 175, 365. SUPPORTED_CHAINS constant removed. All original route handlers intact.

Manually implemented: raw JSON-RPC via fetch (no @solana/web3.js). Uses getSignaturesForAddress + getTransaction. Memo extraction via MemoProgram. SettlementMatch interface shared with evm-detector. ~85 lines.

tasks.description: Supabase migration: add chain column to Invoice table
tasks.context:
Add a `chain` column to the Invoice table in Supabase to record which blockchain the invoice is denominated on.

Create the file: backend/src/migrations/add_chain_to_invoice.sql

Content:
```sql
-- Migration: Add chain field to Invoice table
-- Sprint 10 (multi-chain architecture)
-- Safe to run multiple times (IF NOT EXISTS)

ALTER TABLE "Invoice"
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS chain TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'base';

-- Add check constraint for supported chains
ALTER TABLE "Invoice"
  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS invoice_chain_check;

ALTER TABLE "Invoice"
  ADD CONSTRAINT invoice_chain_check
  CHECK (chain IN ('base', 'polygon', 'solana'));

-- Add index for chain-based queries
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_invoice_chain
  ON "Invoice" (chain);

-- Verify existing rows are defaulted correctly
UPDATE "Invoice" SET chain = 'base' WHERE chain IS NULL;
```

Also create backend/src/migrations/README.md documenting the migration process:
- How to run: paste into Supabase SQL editor
- Rollback: `ALTER TABLE "Invoice" DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS chain;`
- Purpose: enables multi-chain invoicing (Base, Polygon, Solana)

Do NOT actually run the migration — just create the files.

strengths: Idempotent migration with IF NOT EXISTS guards
Proper constraint management and indexing
Clear README with purpose, run steps, and rollback instructions
No markdown code fences in source files

tasks.description: RPC provider evaluation for Polygon + Solana from VPS
tasks.context:
Create docs/rpc-providers.md documenting which RPC providers work from our Hetzner VPS IP. Based on live tests conducted 2026-03-03, here are the actual results:

POLYGON RESULTS (tested via eth_blockNumber):
- https://polygon-bor-rpc.publicnode.com — PASS (returned valid blockNumber)
- https://polygon.gateway.tenderly.co — PASS (returned valid blockNumber)
- https://polygon.meowrpc.com — FAIL (returned JSON-RPC parse error: "Failed to parse request")
- https://1rpc.io/matic — FAIL (curl -sf returned no output / connection failed)

SOLANA RESULTS (tested via getSlot):
- https://api.mainnet-beta.solana.com — PASS (returned slot 403909499)
- https://solana-mainnet.rpc.extrnode.com — FAIL (curl -sf returned no output / connection failed)

Format as a markdown table with columns: Provider, Chain, Status, Notes. Add recommended primary/fallback for each chain. Max 60 lines.

strengths: Correct and concise markdown table matching the spec (Provider, Chain, Status, Notes).
Includes primary and fallback recommendations for each chain.
Well under the 60-line limit with useful contextual notes.

tasks.context:
Create docs/adr/ADR-003-multi-chain-settlement.md — Architecture Decision Record documenting the multi-chain settlement design.

This captures the key decisions made in Sprint 10 so future agents have context.

Structure:
```markdown
# ADR-003: Multi-Chain Settlement Architecture

**Date**: 2026-03-02  
**Status**: Accepted  
**Deciders**: CEO, CTO  

## Context
Invoica must support Base, Polygon, and Solana by March 28.
Stripe x402 only supports Base (EVM). Solana support = competitive moat.

## Decision
[Document the settlement-router pattern, why EVM vs Solana abstraction was separated, why raw JSON-RPC was chosen over viem/solana-web3.js]

## Chain Config
[Document the CHAINS registry, the 3 chains, their USDC addresses]

## Settlement Detection
### EVM (Base + Polygon)
[Document eth_getLogs approach]
### Solana
[Document getSignaturesForAddress approach, memo program for invoice matching]

## Consequences
- Adding a new chain = add entry to chain-registry.ts + implement detector
- Solana requires different address format (base58 vs 0x hex)
- Invoice table needs `chain` column (see migration CHAIN-006)

## Alternatives Considered
- viem library: rejected (adds 200KB dependency, raw RPC is sufficient)
- @solana/web3.js: rejected (ESM-only, causes ts-node issues; raw RPC works)
- The Graph indexer: future consideration for high-volume settlement detection
```

Max 150 lines. Write it as a real ADR a developer would find useful.

review.summary: Manually written: proper markdown ADR, no code fences, complete content covering context/decision/consequences/alternatives. 3-layer architecture documented.
